  • Partner with cross-functional teams across marketing, music, creative, comms, media, and analytics to deliver integrated campaigns
  • Collaborate with artists, labels, and external partners to bring culturally relevant and locally resonant ideas to life
  • Own end-to-end production of experiential campaigns, building clear workflows across stakeholders and ensuring seamless execution
  • Use data and insights to measure campaign performance and continuously optimize audience engagement and reach
  • Create compelling presentations and narratives to communicate strategy, performance, and recommendations to senior stakeholders
  • Manage budgets and resources effectively, ensuring high-quality delivery while maximizing impact

• At least 10+ years of experience in experiential, consumer, or integrated marketing within entertainment, music, or lifestyle brands
  • Proven experience leading end-to-end production of live or experiential campaigns, with the ability to scale for broader impact
  • Experience working with artists, creators, agencies, and cross-functional teams in fast-paced environments
  • Strong ability to balance creativity with operational rigor, bringing ideas to life while managing timelines, budgets, and stakeholders
  • Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple priorities while maintaining clear and effective communication
  • Strong collaborator with the ability to align diverse stakeholders toward a shared goal
  • Fluency in Korean and proficiency in English, with strong communication and storytelling skills
  • Deep understanding of K-pop culture and audience behaviors, with a passion for connecting fans and artists
  • Comfortable using tools such as Google Suite, Excel, PowerPoint, or Keynote to communicate ideas and results

• This role is based in Seoul, Korea
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

jobs.title: Financial Systems Analyst - Platform
jobs.description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

The Oracles sit at the heart of Spotify’s financial infrastructure, building and evolving the systems that power our subledger and accounting lifecycle. From accruals to reconciliation and ledger integrations, the team is driving a broader FinE initiative to standardize and scale how accounting flows across Spotify. You’ll work closely with engineers, finance partners, and product teams to bring clarity, speed, and accuracy to complex financial systems.

raw_data.additionalPlain:
The Canada base range for this position is $109,779.00-156,827.00 CAD plus equity. Benefits available for this position include extended health and dental coverage, retirement savings plans, monthly meal allowance, 23 paid days off, 13 paid flexible holidays, and other benefits in accordance with Canadian employment standards. These ranges and benefits may be modified in the future.


Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
 
At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. lists.content:
• Act as the embedded accounting domain expert within Financial Engineering R&D, contributing to day-to-day product and engineering decisions
  • Guide system and product design to ensure accounting best practices and controls are built into the foundation
  • Translate complex accounting requirements into scalable, structured system inputs across the subledger lifecycle
  • Partner closely with engineers and product teams to shape flows for accruals, adjustments, reconciliation, and rebooking
  • Contribute to the development lifecycle by informing, prototyping, and validating solutions for our core accounting platform
  • Analyze existing accounting flows to separate core requirements from legacy technical constraints
  • Help define and standardize accounting logic across systems like MoneyBooker, IOU Batcher, and NetSuite integrations
  • Break down ambiguous financial and technical challenges into simple, practical solutions

  • You have a strong understanding of accrual-based accounting, subledger processes, and accounting best practices
  • You are experienced in partnering with technical teams and can clearly communicate accounting concepts in a systems context
  • You are comfortable working in data-driven or system-heavy environments and are curious about how systems operate
  • You bring a structured, analytical approach to problem solving and are comfortable navigating ambiguity
  • You have experience working with ERP systems, with NetSuite as a plus
  • You are interested in developing your technical skillset and working more closely with engineering
  • You enjoy working at the intersection of finance, systems, and product in evolving environments

• This role is based in Toronto.
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

jobs.title: Frontend Engineer - Music
jobs.description:
The Rights Systems team builds and operates Spotify Rights Center (SRC), Spotify’s rights management platform that enables the company and its partners to identify, manage, and enforce music licensing rights across creator- and artist-generated content.

Comparable in scope to YouTube’s Content ID, SRC is designed specifically for Spotify’s ecosystem and plays an important role in supporting new formats like video and other emerging content types. Over the past year the team launched SRC from inception to a fully operational production platform, now live and used by real rightsholders.

The platform includes automated content scanning, policy management, enforcement pipelines, dispute workflows, and analytics. The team has grown from a single squad to three squads (approximately 29 engineers, designers, and product specialists) and continues to scale as a strategic investment area for Spotify over the next several years.

lists.content:
• Build and evolve the Spotify Rights Center web application used by music labels, publishers, and other rights holders to manage how their content appears on Spotify
  • Collaborate closely with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to deliver intuitive and high-quality user experiences
  • Develop complex workflows that support large datasets, territory-level permissions, and multi-step processes for managing rights and disputes
  • Integrate frontend systems with multiple backend services to support scalable and reliable data flows
  • Write performant, accessible, and well-tested frontend code using modern development practices
  • Partner with backend engineers to design and evolve APIs that support the needs of the product
  • Contribute to product discovery and ideation with engineers, researchers, and product specialists
  • Help improve engineering practices and advocate for strong frontend architecture and quality standards
  • Support a collaborative team environment that encourages learning, experimentation, and growth

• You have experience building modern web applications using TypeScript and React or a similar framework
  • You are comfortable managing complex application state and data flows
  • You have experience integrating frontend systems with APIs such as REST, gRPC, or GraphQL
  • You understand how to design responsive interfaces that handle loading states, pagination, streaming data, and error handling gracefully
  • You care deeply about creating intuitive and accessible user experiences
  • You have experience building enterprise or B2B applications with complex workflows, permissions, and large datasets
  • You write high-quality code and validate it using modern testing approaches such as unit, integration, or end-to-end testing
  • You take ownership of solutions from requirements through deployment and iteration based on user feedback
  • You enjoy learning new domains and problem spaces and collaborating with teammates to solve meaningful problems

• This role is based in London or Stockholm
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer - Audiobooks
jobs.description:
Spotify for Authors (SFA) is the creator platform for the audiobook industry — a web application that gives publishers, authors, and narrators tools to manage their catalog, track   performance, and distribute content on Spotify. We're scaling toward 100,000 users across a React/TypeScript frontend, Java backend services, and data pipelines that feed into Spotify's   broader audiobook ecosystem.

SFA connects to Spotify's highest-priority bets: expanding audiobook access, building content creation tools, and shaping discovery surfaces. We operate with a "short toes" philosophy —   contribution has no borders, quality is shared, and we use modern tools (including AI) to reduce toil and amplify creativity.

At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
raw_data.descriptionPlain:
Spotify for Authors (SFA) is the creator platform for the audiobook industry — a web application that gives publishers, authors, and narrators tools to manage their catalog, track   performance, and distribute content on Spotify. We're scaling toward 100,000 users across a React/TypeScript frontend, Java backend services, and data pipelines that feed into Spotify's   broader audiobook ecosystem.
SFA connects to Spotify's highest-priority bets: expanding audiobook access, building content creation tools, and shaping discovery surfaces. We operate with a "short toes" philosophy —   contribution has no borders, quality is shared, and we use modern tools (including AI) to reduce toil and amplify creativity.

  • Collaborate closely with engineers, product managers, and designers to ship impactful experiences for audiobook creators
  • Contribute to technical discussions and participate in RFCs and code reviews

  • You're comfortable using AI tools and agents to support your development workflow
  • You're comfortable with modern infrastructure practices (CI/CD, containers, cloud platforms) and care about observability
  • You communicate clearly and collaborate well in a distributed team
  • You're pragmatic and motivated by solving real problems for creators

• This role is based in Stockholm or London
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

jobs.title: Group Product Manager - Messaging
jobs.description:
Messaging is a business-critical system at Spotify. It sits between many message senders and a shared pool of users across several channels (e.g. in-app, push, email, and emerging channels), delivering more than 70 billion impressions per year.

The Messaging team operates within the Freemium Funnel Area, where product, engineering, design, insights, and marketing collaborate to shape how users engage with Spotify across monetization experiences. We are responsible for audience reach, messaging governance, campaign delivery, and the platforms that enable Spotify teams to communicate with users at scale — reliably, responsibly, and effectively.

We have ambitious plans to evolve Messaging from a delivery layer into an intelligent, governed platform that reasons about what to send, to whom, and when — balancing user experience with business outcomes. We are looking for a Group Product Manager to help lead this transformation.

lists.content:
• Set the vision, strategy, and roadmap for Messaging capabilities spanning audience reach, governance, campaign automation, and emerging engagement surfaces
  • Lead and support a group of Product Managers building scalable platform solutions that evolve Messaging from operator-configured execution into intelligent, value-informed decisioning
  • Drive the governance layer for Spotify’s messaging ecosystem by defining how competing business objectives — including conversion, engagement, retention, and upsell — are balanced across channels and user segments
  • Lead the development of message classification systems, cross-domain signal integration, and attribution frameworks that enable continuous learning and optimization
  • Build and protect audience reach through push notification recovery, opt-in optimization, and expansion into new off-platform channels such as WhatsApp
  • Establish observability, monitoring, and alerting capabilities that make messaging performance visible to operators, stakeholders, and leadership
  • Partner closely with engineering, design, and insights teams to deliver high-impact platform capabilities with speed and quality
  • Collaborate with Growth, R&D, Content, and Marketing stakeholders to align priorities, governance principles, escalation paths, and messaging best practices
  • Champion platform thinking across teams by promoting reusable capabilities, enforcing standards, and reducing structural limitations tied to manual configuration
  • Inspire teams with a clear product vision while staying close enough to execution to remove blockers and drive meaningful outcomes

    •

• You have experience building or leading messaging products at consumer scale, ideally in environments where multiple business teams compete for shared user attention
  • You have led product strategy end-to-end, from defining vision through execution and measurable business impact
  • You are experienced navigating ambiguity and connecting governance, infrastructure, and user experience initiatives into a clear strategic direction
  • You know how to use experimentation, user research, attribution modeling, and market insights to inform product decisions
  • You have experience building platform products that support both internal operators and external user experiences
  • You understand the balance between business optimization and protecting user experience, and can design systems that support both
  • You collaborate effectively across product, engineering, design, insights, legal, and executive stakeholders
  • You communicate clearly and can adapt your storytelling to audiences ranging from engineers to senior leadership
  • You support and grow high-performing Product Managers by creating clarity, momentum, and accountability
  • You are comfortable diving into details when needed to unblock teams and accelerate execution
  • You bring a thoughtful perspective on how AI can improve workflows, product thinking, and decision-making responsibly
  • You care deeply about building durable, long-term value over short-term optimization

• This role is based in London, UK or Stockholm, Sweden
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be opportunities for in-person collaboration, while still allowing flexibility to work from home
  • This team operates within the EST and GMT time zones for collaboration

    •

jobs.title: Head of Backstage Marketing
jobs.description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
 
About the Team
Backstage was created at Spotify and has grown into a leading SaaS and open source platform for developer portals. Thousands of companies use Backstage to bring structure to complex microservices environments, improve developer experience, and ship high-quality software faster — including in the age of AI — without losing autonomy or control.Backstage powers centralized software catalogs, standards, and AI enablement capabilities across open source, plugins, and SaaS offerings.
 
Our customers include global enterprises and Fortune 100 companies transforming their engineering culture. This role leads marketing for Shipping Our Expertise (SOE), Spotify’s B2B SaaS business built on our platform capabilities, while also shaping the broader Spotify R&D brand.

Our customers include global enterprises and Fortune 100 companies transforming their engineering culture. This role leads marketing for Shipping Our Expertise (SOE), Spotify’s B2B SaaS business built on our platform capabilities, while also shaping the broader Spotify R&D brand.

lists.content:
• Set and drive the end-to-end marketing strategy for the SOE business across product marketing, growth, and brand
  • Build and execute growth strategies that drive measurable revenue, adoption, and customer expansion
  • Lead digital and paid acquisition programs targeting developers, engineering leaders, and enterprise buyers
  • Define clear positioning and differentiated messaging for technical B2B audiences
  • Partner closely with Engineering, Product, and Sales to align roadmap, go-to-market plans, and commercial priorities
  • Establish and track full-funnel performance across awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and revenue
  • Lead event and community strategy to increase visibility and generate qualified pipeline
  • Scale marketing operations through strong planning, dashboards, and performance reporting
  • Build, mentor, and grow a high-performing, data-informed marketing team

• You have 10+ years of experience in B2B SaaS, growth marketing, or product marketing
  • You have 5+ years of experience leading and developing high-performing teams
  • You have experience marketing developer tools, infrastructure platforms, or technical SaaS products
  • You understand engineering culture and know how to communicate with technical audiences
  • You have owned revenue or adoption targets and can demonstrate measurable business impact
  • You have led digital performance marketing, paid acquisition, and growth funnel optimization
  • You are comfortable working with data, building forecasts, and using dashboards to guide decisions
  • You balance commercial thinking with strong product intuition and user-centered mindset
  • You bring structure and clarity to complex, ambiguous initiatives
  • You thrive in fast-moving environments and enjoy building new approaches from the ground up

• This role is based in Toronto
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

jobs.title: Head of Government Affairs Australia New Zealand
jobs.description:
You will join Spotify’s Global Government Affairs team, working at the intersection of policy, technology, and creativity. This team partners with governments, regulators, and industry groups around the world to help shape a fair, innovative, and inclusive digital ecosystem.

In Australia and New Zealand, you’ll play a key role in representing Spotify’s voice on issues that matter to creators, listeners, and the broader media landscape—ranging from competition and online safety to AI and the future of the creative economy.

raw_data.descriptionPlain:

In Australia and New Zealand, you’ll play a key role in representing Spotify’s voice on issues that matter to creators, listeners, and the broader media landscape—ranging from competition and online safety to AI and the future of the creative economy.

lists.content:
• Lead Spotify’s government affairs strategy across Australia and New Zealand, building trusted relationships with policymakers and stakeholders
  • Represent Spotify in meetings with government officials, regulators, and industry groups across both markets
  • Shape and influence public policy on issues impacting the digital, media, and creator ecosystem
  • Provide clear, strategic guidance to senior leaders on political, legislative, and regulatory developments
  • Partner with Legal, Communications, Music and business teams to align on priorities and messaging
  • Monitor policy trends and proactively identify risks and opportunities for Spotify, creators, and users
  • Develop advocacy strategies and positions that reflect Spotify’s values and support a sustainable, inclusive creative economy
  • Travel regularly between Sydney and Canberra to maintain strong, in-person engagement

• You have significant experience in government affairs, public policy, or a related field across Australia and New Zealand
  • You are experienced in building relationships with government officials, regulators, and industry stakeholders
  • You have a strong understanding of the ANZ political and regulatory landscape, including legislative processes
  • You know how to turn complex policy topics into clear, actionable insights
  • You are comfortable advising senior leaders and contributing to strategic decision-making
  • You care about creating a fair, inclusive, and forward-looking environment for creators, platforms, and users
  • You communicate clearly and represent organizations externally with credibility and authenticity
  • You are able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-moving environment with sound judgment

• This role can be based in Sydney or Canberra

    •

jobs.title: Lead Growth Manager- UK/IE/NL
jobs.description:
You’ll join the Europe Growth team within the Markets & Subscriptions organisation, focused on bringing Spotify to more listeners across the region. This team sits at the intersection of marketing, product, data, and commercial strategy, working closely with local market teams to grow both free and Premium users.

In this role, you’ll own growth across the UK, Ireland, and Netherlands—shaping strategy, running experiments, and turning insights into action that drives meaningful impact.

  • Build and execute CRM and lifecycle campaigns across owned channels such as email, in-app messaging, and push notifications

jobs.title: Lead Music Development, Türkiye
jobs.description: The Lead, Music Development acts as the principal architect in fortifying Spotify’s relationships and standing within the Turkish music landscape across labels, industry bodies, and other institutional stakeholders. Acting as the spokesperson, this role strengthens Spotify’s position in the Turkish music ecosystem by shaping clear narratives and leading strategic initiatives and events. You will address industry sentiment, focus on strengthening institutional relationships, and connect with internal global music leadership to ensure a seamless flow of information and feedback.

You will address industry sentiment, focus on strengthening institutional relationships, and connect with internal global music leadership to ensure a seamless flow of information and feedback.
raw_data.description: The Lead, Music Development acts as the principal architect in fortifying Spotify’s relationships and standing within the Turkish music landscape across labels, industry bodies, and other institutional stakeholders. Acting as the spokesperson, this role strengthens Spotify’s position in the Turkish music ecosystem by shaping clear narratives and leading strategic initiatives and events. You will address industry sentiment, focus on strengthening institutional relationships, and connect with internal global music leadership to ensure a seamless flow of information and feedback.

lists.content:
• Build and lead relationships with major and independent labels, CMOs, and key music industry partners across Türkiye
  • Drive strategic alignment through initiatives like Quarterly Business Reviews, focusing on long-term growth and shared outcomes
  • Represent Spotify as a trusted voice in the Turkish music ecosystem through events, panels, and industry forums
  • Shape and deliver clear, compelling narratives that communicate Spotify’s platform, values, and innovations
  • Partner with Marketing, PR, Government Affairs, and global Music teams to align communication and engagement strategies
  • Provide local teams with insights on market trends, emerging genres, and shifts in listener behavior
  • Translate market data into actionable strategies that support artists, partners, and business growth
  • Design and lead education initiatives that help partners better understand Spotify’s tools, model, and opportunities
  • Define and deliver initiatives that support the growth, diversity, and global reach of Turkish music
  • Manage budgets to execute impactful programs across the local music ecosystem

• You have a minimum of 15 years of experience across media, entertainment, or technology, with exposure to rights, partnerships, or content strategy
  • You understand how the digital music ecosystem works, including streaming models and platform dynamics
  • You know how to build and maintain senior-level relationships across complex stakeholder environments
  • You are comfortable representing an organization publicly and can communicate complex ideas in a clear, engaging way
  • You use market insights and data to guide decisions and influence strategy
  • You collaborate effectively across global teams and can balance local priorities with broader business goals
  • You communicate fluently in both Turkish and English, with confidence in formal presentations and discussions
  • You bring a strong understanding of the Turkish music landscape, including regulatory and industry dynamics
  • You care about supporting creators and contributing to a more inclusive and sustainable music ecosystem

• This role is based in Istanbul
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

jobs.title: Lead, User Growth Optimization
jobs.description:
We are seeking a Lead, User Growth Optimization to elevate our conversion optimization strategy. This role involves both implementing current optimization initiatives and developing future conversion strategies and hypotheses. The successful candidate will report to the User Growth Optimization Director within the Growth team, focusing on increasing subscriptions, reducing churn, and maximizing lifetime value.

Join the world’s fastest-growing online subscription business in a high-impact role where you will put a personal mark on the success of Spotify by delivering even faster subscriber growth.

raw_data.additionalPlain:
Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
 
At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.

raw_data.description:
We are seeking a Lead, User Growth Optimization to elevate our conversion optimization strategy. This role involves both implementing current optimization initiatives and developing future conversion strategies and hypotheses. The successful candidate will report to the User Growth Optimization Director within the Growth team, focusing on increasing subscriptions, reducing churn, and maximizing lifetime value.

Join the world’s fastest-growing online subscription business in a high-impact role where you will put a personal mark on the success of Spotify by delivering even faster subscriber growth.

lists.content:
• Drive conversion optimization across the full user funnel, with a clear focus on subscriptions, retention, and lifetime value.
  • Develop bold, data-informed experimentation hypotheses based on quantitative analysis, user behavior, and research insights.
  • Design, build, and iterate on A/B and multivariate experiments that influence messaging, design, localization, personalization, and user journeys.
  • Implement front-end experiments using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript on Spotify’s web surfaces.
  • Work across a modern tech stack, including React and Next.js, to ensure experiments are robust, scalable, and performant.
  • Deploy and manage experiments using Spotify’s in-house experimentation platform and selected third-party testing tools.
  • Partner closely with R&D teams on implementation details and technical alignment.
  • Collaborate with marketing, growth, and business stakeholders to support campaigns, market initiatives, and specific traffic strategies.
  • Maintain an agile optimization workflow with continuous testing, learning, and iteration.

• You have 5+ years of experience working with conversion rate optimization, experimentation, and A/B testing methodologies.
  • You bring a strong strategic and analytical mindset, using data to guide decisions and shape hypotheses.
  • You are confident coding front-end experiments using JavaScript, HTML, and CSS in production environments.
  • You have experience working with modern JavaScript frameworks such as React (experience with Angular or similar frameworks is a plus).
  • You have hands-on experience with commercial experimentation or personalization tools such as Optimizely, Adobe Target, or Google Optimize.
  • You’ve worked in online consumer products, digital subscriptions, e-commerce, media, or fast-moving tech environments.
  • You are comfortable collaborating in shared codebases and using version control tools.
  • You are organized, proactive, and able to keep complex initiatives moving forward across multiple stakeholders.
  • You communicate fluently in English and enjoy working with international teams and global markets.

• This role is based in London.
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

jobs.title: Legal Counsel - Commerce & Subscriptions
jobs.description: You’ll join Spotify’s Commerce & Subscriptions Legal team, part of the broader Products and Services Legal team, supporting a fast-moving part of the business focused on how users purchase, manage, and experience paid offerings. This team partners closely with product, engineering, design, and business stakeholders to enable innovative e-commerce experiences while managing legal risk in a thoughtful, pragmatic way.

raw_data.description: You’ll join Spotify’s Commerce & Subscriptions Legal team, part of the broader Products and Services Legal team, supporting a fast-moving part of the business focused on how users purchase, manage, and experience paid offerings. This team partners closely with product, engineering, design, and business stakeholders to enable innovative e-commerce experiences while managing legal risk in a thoughtful, pragmatic way.

lists.content:
• Provide practical, commercially-focused legal advice to product managers, engineers, designers, and business partners building and scaling e-commerce products and services.
  • Support the development of new monetisation models including subscription add-ons, one-time purchases, and affiliate sales channels
  • Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial agreements with external partners
  • Advise on consumer-facing product features, including UX flows, pricing structures, and terms of service to ensure compliance with applicable laws
  • Partner with cross-functional teams exploring new ways to grow Spotify’s commerce and subscriptions offerings
  • Collaborate closely with other Legal teams to deliver consistent, joined-up legal guidance across products and services
  • Build a strong understanding of Spotify’s subscriptions business, payments systems, and customer support operations
    Contribute to scalable legal resources such as playbooks, templates, and training materials to support a growing business

• You are qualified to practice law in the UK or an EU member state with 4+ years of relevant experience working in a law firm and/or in-house legal department
  • You have experience advising on e-commerce, digital consumer transactions and/or subscription-based products
  • You're well-versed in online commerce law, including consumer protection and payments regulation and understand digital content regulations and data privacy frameworks
  • You communicate complex legal concepts clearly and effectively to non-legal audiences
  • You are comfortable working across a variety of legal topics and enjoy learning new areas of the business. You are comfortable with ambiguity and change
  • You build strong relationships and collaborate effectively across teams
  • You bring sound judgment and a pragmatic approach to balancing legal risk with business opportunity

• This role is based in London
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. We ask that you come in 2-3 times per week.

jobs.title: Legal Counsel - Licensing & Business Development
jobs.description: You’ll join Spotify’s Legal Licensing and Business Development team, supporting partnerships that bring Spotify to listeners around the world. This team works at the intersection of technology, media, and business, helping shape how Spotify is distributed across platforms, devices, and experiences. You’ll collaborate closely with business development, finance, and licensing teams to support innovative deals that expand Spotify’s global reach.

You’ll collaborate closely with business development, finance, and licensing teams to support innovative deals that expand Spotify’s global reach.

lists.content:
• Partner with Business Development teams to support partnerships that enable Spotify distribution across platforms, devices, and services
  • Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial agreements, including technology, AI, hardware, and distribution partnerships
  • Provide clear, practical legal guidance on complex deal structures, risks, and regulatory considerations
    Collaborate with cross-functional partners across finance, accounting, and licensing to support agreement execution and implementation
  • Translate legal concepts into clear guidance that informs business strategy and decision-making
  • Help improve and scale contract templates, processes, and ways of working to support global growth
  • Support teams in navigating internal policies, approval processes, and evolving legal frameworks

• You have a law degree from an accredited university and are qualified to practice law in the UK or Sweden
  • You have 3+ years of post-qualification experience in a law firm and/or in-house legal team
  • You are experienced in drafting and negotiating commercial agreements, particularly in technology, media, AI, or IP-focused environments
  • You are comfortable working across global teams and time zones, managing multiple stakeholders and priorities
  • You communicate complex legal concepts in a clear, accessible way for non-legal partners
  • You bring a practical, solution-oriented approach to problem-solving and decision-making
  • You have experience supporting business development or partnership-driven organizations
  • You are curious about emerging technologies and understand the legal considerations around AI and digital distribution
  • You collaborate effectively and take ownership of your work while contributing to a supportive team environment

• This role is based in London or Stockholm.
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

jobs.title: Legal Counsel - Platform Liability
jobs.description:
Spotify’s Legal team is growing. We’re facing innovative challenges that mirror the ground-breaking industry and product at the heart of our company. As we’ve expanded into more than 184 countries and our company has grown tremendously, we find ourselves facing new and interesting legal issues every day.

As Legal Counsel, Platform Liability & Enforcement at Spotify, you will help supervise Spotify’s legal takedown practices and policies and you will provide legal support for the teams that develop, implement, and support Spotify’s content policies. You will also work directly to resolve claims related to these policies and help build out processes for scale. Your experience making risk-based assessments and proactively finding solutions to complex problems will be crucial.

Spotify’s Legal team is growing. We’re facing innovative challenges that mirror the ground-breaking industry and product at the heart of our company. As we’ve expanded into more than 184 countries and our company has grown tremendously, we find ourselves facing new and interesting legal issues every day.
As Legal Counsel, Platform Liability & Enforcement at Spotify, you will help supervise Spotify’s legal takedown practices and policies and you will provide legal support for the teams that develop, implement, and support Spotify’s content policies. You will also work directly to resolve claims related to these policies and help build out processes for scale. Your experience making risk-based assessments and proactively finding solutions to complex problems will be crucial.
raw_data.description:
Spotify’s Legal team is growing. We’re facing innovative challenges that mirror the ground-breaking industry and product at the heart of our company. As we’ve expanded into more than 184 countries and our company has grown tremendously, we find ourselves facing new and interesting legal issues every day.

As Legal Counsel, Platform Liability & Enforcement at Spotify, you will help supervise Spotify’s legal takedown practices and policies and you will provide legal support for the teams that develop, implement, and support Spotify’s content policies. You will also work directly to resolve claims related to these policies and help build out processes for scale. Your experience making risk-based assessments and proactively finding solutions to complex problems will be crucial.

lists.content:
• Advise on Spotify’s global compliance with platform liability frameworks and content safe harbor laws (e.g., Digital Services Act, DMCA, Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act)
  • Support managing Spotify’s legal takedown process, across content types, across all types of legal issues including intellectual property and speech-based offenses
  • Provide legal advice across Spotify on platform liability, including issue-spotting and strategizing solutions for exciting new products
  • Work with internal and external counsel and government affairs experts to track evolving legal requirements across the territories where Spotify operates
  • Counsel the teams responsible for building, managing, and implementing Spotify’s content policies, including the Trust & Safety team and the Content Protection team
  • Work on proactive enforcement efforts regarding potential abuse of the Spotify platform
  • Provide legal support for litigations and press inquiries arising out of content takedown issues
  • Work with operations teams within and outside the legal team to develop efficient, scalable practices around legal takedown requests
  • Perform rotational work as business requires

• Law degree (or equivalent)
  • 5+ years of experience working in a law firm and/or in-house legal department
  • Deep understanding of intermediary liability frameworks, with a preference for experience litigating cases involving intermediary liability
  • Proven expertise in copyright law and other content-related legal issues
  • Experience assessing and advising on a broad set of legal standards, even when those standards are new to you
  • Experience in partnering with global stakeholders
  • A passion for legal problems; you can explain legal concepts to non-lawyers
  • A problem solver who stays calm when problems come your way
  • A builder; you can work collaboratively (especially with non-lawyers) on finding practical, implementable tools and operational solutions
  • Self-motivated and can identify and complete work to be done without being instructed
  • A multi-tasker who can triage to make sure the right matters get your attention
  • You’re willing to really dig deep on projects
  • You work well as part of a team — both with other lawyers and with non-legal stakeholders

•  This role is based in London
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home

jobs.title: Manager, Partner Product and Delivery
jobs.description: We are looking for a Manager, Partner Product and Delivery, who will join our team spread across the globe, and help manage Spotify's important Premium Partnerships projects in Latin America. You will have the operational and day-to-day responsibility to manage the technical implementation and performance of the partnerships. You will report directly to the Associate Director, Partner Product and Delivery.

raw_data.description: We are looking for a Manager, Partner Product and Delivery, who will join our team spread across the globe, and help manage Spotify's important Premium Partnerships projects in Latin America. You will have the operational and day-to-day responsibility to manage the technical implementation and performance of the partnerships. You will report directly to the Associate Director, Partner Product and Delivery.

lists.content:
• Participate in initial discussions with potential partners, explaining how partners can distribute premium offerings in different ways.
  • Work with the business team on the technical setup of the partnership.
  • Ensure that Spotify's commitments in a partner relationship are delivered, as well as follow-up on the partner's commitments.
  • Document all actions with partners for keeping track of changes.
  • Develop our tooling together with R&D to make sure we can scale the business.

• Documented working experience within telecom, Internet start-up, media or the digital music industry.
  • Proficient in written and spoken English and Spanish.
  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent experience.
  • You are proficient with REST APIs, SQL and big data analytics such as BigQuery, product and project tools such as JIRA and Github, productivity tools such as Gemini, ChatGPT, and common understanding of AI functionality, Unix and Shell scripting.
  • Experience in writing software in one or more languages, such as Java or Python.
  • A self-motivated can-do demeanor, you won’t be afraid to step in when needed.
  • You understand that collaboration and team play is key to success.
  • Strong presentation skills and shown success in working directly with partners.
  • A consistent track record in technical leadership, explaining complex relations both to technical and business people.
  • Experience working under deadlines and prioritizing multiple projects simultaneously.
  • Willing to travel, mainly Latin America.

• For this role, the preferred locations are Mexico City, or Sao Paolo. We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.
  • This team operates within the Eastern Standard time zone for collaboration.

jobs.title: Manager, Production - Brazil
jobs.description:
Spotify’s Marketing team in Latin America helps shape how Spotify shows up across the region through culture, creativity, and storytelling. We create work that feels authentic, locally relevant, and connected to the artists, creators, and fans who make Spotify unique.

Based in São Paulo, and working closely with teams across Latin America, this role will lead production for brand initiatives in Brazil, ensuring creative ideas are delivered with excellence from concept through execution. You’ll combine strong creative judgment with operational expertise to bring impactful work to life while balancing quality, timelines, and budgets.

lists.content:
• Lead end-to-end production for Spotify’s brand and marketing initiatives in Brazil, from initial scoping and planning through final delivery.
  • Oversee production across multiple formats, including video, photography, live experiences, events, digital content, and on-platform activations.

  • Create structure around project intake, prioritization, and resource planning to help teams focus on the highest-impact work.
  • Build and manage relationships with production companies, creative vendors, directors, artists, designers, and other external partners.
  • Identify culturally relevant collaborators who help elevate Spotify’s creative work and strengthen local market relevance.
  • Guide talent and partner selection by balancing creative ambition, operational excellence, scalability, and budget considerations.

raw_data.descriptionPlain:
The Marketing Team in Latin America plays a meaningful role in defining and driving Spotify’s Brand in the region. Marketing Managers in Mexico are responsible for the Marketing activities in the country. The position reports into the Mexico Marketing Lead.
 
We are seeking an outstanding marketer who is passionate about music culture, with a proven track record of driving innovative social marketing strategies and initiatives, fostering significant partnerships, and delivering impactful, cross-functional campaigns and content.
 
The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced environment, excels at collaborating across diverse teams and markets, and is deeply committed to crafting culturally resonant initiatives for young music fans to propel Spotify’s business in the country.
 
The Social Marketing Manager will be responsible for developing, localizing and implementing Spotify’s marketing strategy in Mexico to drive brand awareness and user engagement.
 
This individual will play a pivotal role in positioning the platform as a cultural powerhouse, fostering strong connections with audiences, and ensuring our brand remains at the forefront of the music industry.
 
You’ll collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, including the Global Marketing Team, Music, PR & Comms, Growth and Podcasts, to craft campaigns that resonate with diverse audiences and align with our brand vision.

jobs.title: Manager, Technical Advertising Solutions - EMEA
jobs.department: Advertising Sales - Emerging and Scaled Sales
jobs.description:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 

We're looking for a Manager, Technical Advertising Solutions to lead a team responsible for ensuring the successful delivery of advertising campaigns across Spotify's Ads business. You'll partner closely with Sales, Product, Engineering, buyers, and DSP partners to solve complex technical challenges across our Programmatic and Direct advertising products, improve campaign performance, and help scale a world-class advertising platform.

The Manager, Technical Advertising Solutions will serve as the operational and technical lead for their regional team — driving quality, optimizing campaign delivery, and fostering strong partnerships with internal and external stakeholders. This role works in close coordination with our offshore support teams and reports to the Head of Global Advertising Solutions (Programmatic & Direct).

We're looking for a Manager, Technical Advertising Solutions to lead a team responsible for ensuring the successful delivery of advertising campaigns across Spotify's Ads business. You'll partner closely with Sales, Product, Engineering, buyers, and DSP partners to solve complex technical challenges across our Programmatic and Direct advertising products, improve campaign performance, and help scale a world-class advertising platform.

The Manager, Technical Advertising Solutions will serve as the operational and technical lead for their regional team — driving quality, optimizing campaign delivery, and fostering strong partnerships with internal and external stakeholders. This role works in close coordination with our offshore support teams and reports to the Head of Global Advertising Solutions (Programmatic & Direct).

lists.content:
• Lead and manage a regional team of troubleshooting and QA professionals focused on programmatic and direct advertising campaigns
  • Partner with Product and Sales Training teams to support new product launches, identify skill gaps, and build scalable training programs and operational documentation.
  • Work cross-functionally with Sales, Product, and Engineering teams to optimize digital advertising campaign delivery and resolve technical issues
  • Partner with buyers and DSP partners to troubleshoot and resolve campaign delivery challenges
  • Lead collaboration with global support teams to deliver high-quality campaign operations and consistent customer outcomes
  • Own the regional ticket-based support workflow, maintaining quality and SLA standards across the team
  • Drive structured QA processes and develop best practices for programmatic and direct ad campaign troubleshooting
  • Serve as a regional escalation point for complex technical issues impacting ad delivery and campaign performance
  • Identify recurring campaign and platform issues and partner with Product, Engineering, and Ad Product Quality teams to drive long-term solutions.

• You have 5+ years of experience in digital advertising, including 3+ years working with programmatic advertising solutions.
  • You have experience leading, coaching, and developing high-performing technical or operational teams.; experience partnering with globally distributed support teams is a plus
  • You have experience in Ad Operations at a Publisher, SSP, and/or DSP — DSP experience is a strong plus
  • You have experience building strong cross-functional relationships across go-to-market teams such as Sales and Customer Success
  • You have strong technical knowledge of the digital ads ecosystem, including ad serving, programmatic buying, campaign delivery, and measurement
  • You have experience working on a ticket-based support or operations system
  • You have customer service or client-facing experience, with a track record of managing stakeholder expectations under pressure
  • You are highly adaptable to new systems, platforms, and technologies
  • You have strong knowledge of the broader digital advertising industry landscape, including DSPs, SSPs, DMPs, and ad verification tools

• This role is based in London, UK
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. This team operates across our GMT and EST time zones for collaboration.
    •

jobs.title: Marketing Manager UK/IE
jobs.description:
We have an exciting opportunity for an experienced Marketing Manager to join the team at a time of significant strategic ambition. Working as one with teams across the business, to plan, and deliver high-impact marketing campaigns across culture, music, and content, helping fans connect deeper to the artists and creators they love both on and off the platform.

This role requires expertise in creative campaign ideation, end-to-end XFN campaign leadership, live event and content production, multi-channel marketing, and the ability to translate cultural moments and releases into innovative and impactful fan experiences and content.

jobs.title: Marketing Manager, Brand Experience
jobs.description:
About the team
You’ll join Spotify’s Central Europe Marketing team, part of our Markets organization, focused on building meaningful connections with audiences across Germany, Austria, and Switzerland for 1 year fixed term in Berlin!  This team brings Spotify to life through culturally relevant campaigns, brand activations, and live experiences that resonate deeply with local music and youth culture. jobs.title: Music Editor, Türkiye
jobs.description:
The Music Mission team owns Spotify’s end to end proposition for music creators and the experiences they create for fans. The team is dedicated to building tools and services to enable creation, promotion, expression, and monetization at scale.

In Spotify, the Editorial team shapes how millions of listeners discover and connect with music every day. In this role, you’ll help define Spotify’s editorial strategy for Türkiye—bringing together deep cultural knowledge and data-informed programming to create playlists and experiences that feel timely, relevant, and distinctly local.

Currently the team is looking for a passionate and knowledgeable music professional to join our team in Dubai. If you have an expert ear for music, a deep understanding of the Turkish culture and music ecosystem, and a strong ability to blend data-driven insights with cultural expertise, we’d love to hear from you.

In this role, you’ll curate and shape the music listening experience, programming playlists across moods, moments, and genres. You’ll play a key role in developing our editorial strategy for Türkiye while keeping a pulse on emerging trends, rising artists, and cultural moments. Your work will directly impact how audiences engage with music on Spotify.

lists.content:

  • Develop and co-create editorial strategies for Türkiye
  • Analyze platform data to refine and improve playlist programming and overall editorial performance

  • Translate music and cultural knowledge into engaging, relevant content for Spotify users

  • Use cultural expertise to innovate and expand Spotify’s programming efforts and local initiatives

  • Fluent in both Turkish and English, with excellent written and spoken proficiency
  • A growth mindset and a collaborative spirit

• This role is based in Dubai, UAE

jobs.title: Partner Marketing Manager
jobs.description:
Spotify is looking for a creative, commercially minded, and highly motivated Partner Marketing Manager to join our India Marketing team. In this role, you will develop and execute innovative marketing partnerships that help drive revenue growth, strengthen Spotify’s cultural relevance, and create meaningful value for both Spotify and our partners.

You will identify and build relationships with brands that align with Spotify’s strategic priorities across Premium, Brand, Product, and Music. Working across multiple functions, you will develop compelling partnership concepts and oversee their execution from idea through launch and measurement. This is a highly collaborative role that sits within the Marketing team and operates closely with Ad Sales to unlock new growth opportunities for Spotify India.

lists.content:
• Lead the development of partnership marketing strategies that drive business growth and strengthen Spotify’s market position
  • Build business cases, projections, success metrics, and operational plans for partnership opportunities
  • Identify and evaluate partner brands that align with Spotify’s strategic priorities and commercial objectives
  • Develop innovative and culturally relevant partnership concepts that create value for Spotify, partners, artists, and fans
  • Manage partnership budgets and project timelines, ensuring efficient use of resources and strong return on investment
  • Partner with creative agencies to develop and execute integrated marketing campaigns across content, social, events, and influencer activations
  • Provide creative direction and quality oversight across partnership assets to ensure they align with Spotify’s brand and campaign objectives
  • Collaborate closely with Music, Editorial, Brand Marketing, Content, Media, PR, Sales, and Corporate Communications teams to deliver seamless execution
  • Partner with Legal, FP&A, Licensing, and other stakeholders to ensure partnerships are compliant, viable, and operationally feasible
  • Analyze campaign performance and partnership outcomes, providing recommendations and insights to improve future initiatives
  • Influence partner marketing plans through strategic guidance, audience insights, and campaign optimization recommendations
  • Build trusted, long-term relationships with key partner brands and identify new opportunities for growth and collaboration
  • Assess and mitigate risks throughout the partnership lifecycle while maintaining strong execution standards
  • Work closely with the Sales team to identify and develop new partnership opportunities that contribute to revenue growth

• You have 5+ years of experience in brand marketing, partnerships, sponsorships, or integrated marketing
  • You have experience across multiple marketing disciplines, including digital content, social media, and live events
  • You have a strong understanding of brand strategy and a proven ability to build impactful marketing partnerships
  • You are a creative thinker who enjoys developing innovative ideas and testing new approaches
  • You are comfortable managing multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced environment
  • You have experience working cross-functionally with both internal and external stakeholders
  • You know how to manage budgets, evaluate performance, and track return on investment
  • You bring strong project management skills and consistently deliver high-quality work against ambitious timelines
  • You are skilled at balancing creativity, commercial objectives, and operational considerations when making decisions
  • You communicate clearly and confidently, even in complex or challenging situations
  • You build trust quickly and maintain strong relationships with partners and stakeholders
  • You understand the importance of governance, legal compliance, and operational processes in partnership marketing
  • You are comfortable working in a matrixed organization and collaborating across multiple business functions

• This role is based in Mumbai, India
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

jobs.title: Principal Product Manager - ML / Subscriptions Growth
jobs.description:
Spotify’s mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators.
 
The Subscriptions Mission builds and evolves Spotify’s subscription products and marketplace experiences to drive sustainable user and revenue growth globally. We focus on awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization strategies that help users unlock the full value of Spotify while enabling the business to scale efficiently and responsibly.
 
User Understanding sits within Subscriptions and focuses on building the intelligence layer that makes our growth efforts smarter—through decisioning, data signals, and ML models that power personalization across surfaces and lifecycle moments. You’ll work at the intersection of machine learning innovation and commercial strategy, partnering closely with ML engineers, data scientists, data platform teams, product insights, and business stakeholders to shape high-impact growth initiatives.

raw_data.descriptionPlain:
Spotify’s mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators.
 
The Subscriptions Mission builds and evolves Spotify’s subscription products and marketplace experiences to drive sustainable user and revenue growth globally. We focus on awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization strategies that help users unlock the full value of Spotify while enabling the business to scale efficiently and responsibly.

raw_data.description:
Spotify’s mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators.
 
The Subscriptions Mission builds and evolves Spotify’s subscription products and marketplace experiences to drive sustainable user and revenue growth globally. We focus on awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization strategies that help users unlock the full value of Spotify while enabling the business to scale efficiently and responsibly.
 
User Understanding sits within Subscriptions and focuses on building the intelligence layer that makes our growth efforts smarter—through decisioning, data signals, and ML models that power personalization across surfaces and lifecycle moments. You’ll work at the intersection of machine learning innovation and commercial strategy, partnering closely with ML engineers, data scientists, data platform teams, product insights, and business stakeholders to shape high-impact growth initiatives.

lists.content:
• Set the product vision and multi-quarter strategy for ML- and AI-driven subscriber growth across awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization. These strategies will define and inform how we develop data signal sourcing, data platform capabilities, and model validation.
  • Own a portfolio of initiatives end-to-end, aligning multiple squads and stakeholders around clear goals, sequencing, and measurable outcomes (conversion, churn, ARPU, LTV).
  • Turn behavioral and revenue signals into crisp hypotheses, define success metrics and guardrails, and drive an experimentation roadmap that’s prioritized by impact and learning value.
  • Establish strong measurement practices (A/B testing, incrementality, causal inference where appropriate), and ensure we scale only what demonstrates durable lift against strong baselines.
  • Set direction to prioritize and develop data signals, attributes, data products, and feature store requirements
  • Drive lifecycle and personalization strategies (including trial design, offer targeting, win-back, and plan nudges) that balance user value, revenue outcomes, and long-term trust.
  • Partner with pricing and packaging stakeholders to design experiments that improve monetization while remaining transparent, locally relevant, and sustainable.
  • Make pragmatic decisions about when to use ML, GenAI, heuristics, or rules, balancing ROI, complexity, latency, maintainability, and launch risk. Start with simple solutions before investing in complex systems.
  • Ensure production readiness for ML-backed experiences through monitoring, performance evaluation, guardrails, data quality standards, and rollout plans that work at global scale.
  • Communicate trade-offs and insights clearly to senior leadership, translating technical complexity into business impact and crisp decisions.
  • Elevate product craft across the mission by mentoring other PMs, strengthening decision frameworks, and building a culture of learning, accountability, and inclusive collaboration.

• You bring 8+ years of product management experience, including leading data-driven or ML-backed products from discovery through global rollout.
  • You have led platform or shared-capability product work where driving alignment and adoption was key to success, not just shipping features
  • You have strong technical fluency in experimentation design, A/B testing, causal inference concepts, ML fundamentals, recommender/personalization systems, and data infrastructure (feature stores, attribute systems, data pipelines, data governance)..
  • You have experience defining and governing shared data products or data layers - establishing standards and processes
  • You consistently connect product decisions to measurable business outcomes, with a track record of improving conversion, retention, churn, ARPU, and/or LTV.
  • You have experience in subscription or e-commerce business models, including lifecycle optimization, offers, pricing, packaging, or marketplace dynamics.
  • You demonstrate strong judgment in choosing the simplest effective solution, and you know when advanced ML or GenAI is worth the trade-offs. You resist over-engineering and insist on proving incremental value.
  • You build responsibly, considering fairness, bias mitigation, privacy, and localization — especially when personalization affects pricing, offers, or eligibility.
  • You lead with clarity and empathy, give and receive feedback well, and raise the bar through coaching and principled decision-making.
  • You influence without authority across engineering, data science, design, analytics, finance, and commercial stakeholders, and you’re skilled at aligning teams through ambiguity. You are experienced with resolving trade-offs between competing priorities.

• This role is based in London, United Kingdom or Stockholm, Sweden.
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

jobs.title: Product Designer - Core Experience (Core Value & Engagement)
jobs.description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.
 
We’re looking for mid-level to senior product designers with strong visual design skills to join the team focusing on the core mobile experience. This team works on some of the most essential parts of the Spotify experience that shapes some of Spotify’s most iconic and emotionally resonant experiences - the features that help people connect with music, organize their libraries, and engage more deeply with what they love. You might be designing how users share and discover music with friends, how they create customise and manage playlists, or Whether it’s evolving how users organize their music, creating new ways to share audio socially, or integrating AI to help users find their next favorite song, you will work on the products that define the Core Spotify experience.
 
It’s a high-impact, fast-moving space where priorities evolve as we explore new ways to bring users closer to the content they care about. As a designer here, you’ll have the opportunity to shape foundational experiences that millions of people use every day.This is a role for a designer who thrives on variety, moves quickly, and wants to see their work impact millions of listeners across multiple surfaces

lists.content:
• Contribute to the design quality of high-priority features across the teams portfolio, moving between projects as product strategies evolve.
  • Apply Spotify's design principles to generate thoughtful, user-centered solutions grounded in clear problem statements, constraints, and team goals.
  • Design across core Spotify surfaces that drive long-term engagement, retention, and user satisfaction.
  • Work on a range of experiences—from social sharing to playlist curation and library organization—adapting as priorities evolve.
  • Frame design problems clearly by defining user needs, success criteria, constraints, and tradeoffs.
  • Explore multiple concepts and directions, knowing when to go deep and when to pivot based on insights and feedback.
  • Collaborate closely with product, engineering, research, and data partners to bring ideas to life.
  • Leverage AI-powered tools to explore ideas, synthesize insights, prototype, and improve design efficiency and quality.
  • Take ownership of the end-to-end design process, from early exploration through to final execution and iteration.
  • Contribute to the broader design community by sharing work, feedback, and learnings.

• You have experience designing mobile consumer products and can contribute independently to end-to-end design work.
  • You know how to craft thoughtful interaction design, information architecture, and user journeys.
  • You are comfortable working across ambiguity and can balance multiple product areas or shifting priorities.
  • You care about understanding user needs and grounding your decisions in research and data.
  • You have experience designing for mobile platforms and understand iOS and Android conventions.
  • You are curious about personalization, AI, and how intelligent systems shape user experiences and trust.
  • You communicate your design decisions clearly and can connect user needs to product outcomes.
  • You enjoy collaborating with cross-functional partners and actively seek feedback to improve your work.
  • You are comfortable using AI-enabled tools as part of your workflow.
  • You have a portfolio that demonstrates your design process, thinking, and impact.

• This role is based in London or Stockholm.
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

jobs.title: Research Scientist - Music
jobs.description:
We are seeking a Research Scientist to join our Artist-First AI Music lab. Our team pioneers and advances state-of-the-art generative technologies for music that create breakthrough experiences for fans and artists. We invent entirely new listening experiences that center and celebrate artists and creatives. All of our products will put artists and songwriters first, through these four principles:
 
Partnerships with record labels, distributors, and music publishers: We’ll develop new products for artists and fans through upfront agreements, not by asking for forgiveness later.
Choice in participation: We recognize there’s a wide range of views on use of generative music tools within the artistic community. Therefore, artists and rightsholders will choose if and how to participate to ensure the use of AI tools aligns with the values of the people behind the music.
Fair compensation and new revenue: We will build products that create wholly new revenue streams for rightsholders, artists, and songwriters, ensuring they are properly compensated for uses of their work and transparently credited for their contributions.
Artist-fan connection: AI tools we develop will not replace human artistry. They will give artists new ways to be creative and connect with fans. We will leverage our role as the place where more than 700 million people already come to listen to music every month to ensure that generative AI deepens artist-fan connections.
 
For more information, see this press release: https://newsroom.spotify.com/2025-10-16/artist-first-ai-music-spotify-collaboration/

Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
 
At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.

lists.content:
• Conduct ground breaking research in music generation (diffusion, flow matching, or autoregressive models), as well as related domains like ML-based audio processing, music information retrieval, machine learning, and signal processing.
  • Run large-scale experiments with access to Spotify’s extensive infrastructure and an audience of more than 700 million monthly active users.
  • Create practical applications that harness generative technologies, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in listening experiences.
  • Collaborate as part of a cross-functional team—working closely with scientists, engineers, product managers, designers, user researchers, and analysts—to craft innovative solutions to complex challenges.
  • Have a direct impact on Spotify’s products, tools, and services, working on projects that influence the entire organization.
  • Engage with the broader research community by publishing your findings, delivering talks, and attending top conferences.

• You have a Ph.D. degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, or a related field. Previous industry experience is helpful.
  • You have experience in one or more of the following fields: generative modeling, machine learning, music information retrieval, speech processing, audio processing, signal processing, probabilistic modeling, computer vision, or related areas.
  • You have publications in communities such as ICASSP, ISMIR, INTERSPEECH, ICLR, AAAI, IJCAI, NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, ECCV, ICCV, or related.
  • You have strong coding skills in the following languages/libraries: Python, PyTorch, NumPy.
  • You are a creative problem solver who is passionate about building outstanding products that add real value to millions of people.
  • You are enthusiastic about learning more about turning research ideas into products operating at scale.
  • You can explain complex topics in simple terms, and you enjoy building strong relationships with colleagues and stakeholders.

• We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, you can be within the EMEA region as long as we have a work location
  • This team operates within the Central European and GMT time zone for collaboration.
  • Core working hours are CET 3pm-6pm / EST 9am-12pm.

jobs.title: Sales Trainer, LATAM
jobs.description:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 

Our Ad Sales Training & Enablement team helps Spotify’s global Sales organization perform at its best. We design and deliver learning experiences that keep our sellers confident, informed, and customer-focused. Working closely with Sales Leadership, Product Marketing, and Business Marketing, we build programs that support growth across Spotify’s evolving advertising business.

So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
 
At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. lists.content:
• Partner with sales leaders to shape and deliver training strategies for LATAM teams, with a focus on Mexico and Brazil
  • Lead engaging onboarding sessions for new hires, both virtually and in person
  • Collaborate with Product Marketing and Business Marketing to enable teams on new ad products and positioning
  • Design and facilitate interactive workshops that build core sales and account management skills
  • Assess learning needs and create clear, practical training materials that support day-to-day work
  • Provide coaching that helps sales team members grow their confidence and impact
  • Measure program effectiveness and use insights to continuously improve learning experiences

• You have 2+ years of experience in sales training, enablement, or a related field
  • You understand the digital media landscape, including programmatic advertising and/or social platforms
  • You know how to facilitate sessions that are engaging, inclusive, and tailored to different learning styles
  • You have experience building end-to-end training programs, from needs assessment to delivery
  • You build strong relationships with stakeholders across teams and levels
  • You communicate feedback clearly and supportively, helping others grow
  • You stay organized and manage multiple projects across regions with ease
  • You have experience in sales or media buying
  • You are fluent in Spanish and English, and you have working proficiency in Portuguese
  • Resume must be in English

• .This role is based in Sao Paulo
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. We ask that you come in 2 times per week.

jobs.title: Security Engineer- Product Security
jobs.description:
Security engineers at Spotify help protect the security of our platform and the experience of more than 700 million users around the world. We're looking for an experienced engineer to join us in securing some of Spotify’s most important engineering initiatives.

You’ll work across product and infrastructure security, partnering with autonomous development and platform teams to build secure systems at scale. We’re a distributed team that combines deep security expertise with practical tooling and guidance, helping engineers move quickly while continuously strengthening Spotify’s security posture. We value curiosity, collaboration, and a willingness to both teach and learn from others.

You bring experience in security, software, or systems engineering and enjoy solving complex technical challenges. In this role, you’ll represent security across a variety of engineering and business contexts, so you’re comfortable communicating with diverse audiences and translating security concepts into practical outcomes.
